root_text.an3.73:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.an3.73:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā gilānā vuṭṭhito hoti aciravuṭṭhito gelaññā.
root_text.an3.73:1.4: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.73:1.5: “dīgharattāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānāmi:
root_text.an3.73:1.7: Samādhi nu kho, bhante, pubbe, pacchā ñāṇaṁ;
root_text.an3.73:1.11: Ayañca mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ atigambhīraṁ pañhaṁ pucchati.
root_text.an3.73:1.12: Yannūnāhaṁ mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ ekamantaṁ apanetvā dhammaṁ deseyyan”ti.
root_text.an3.73:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ bāhāyaṁ gahetvā ekamantaṁ apanetvā mahānāmaṁ sakkaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.73:2.2: “sekhampi kho, mahānāma, sīlaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā, asekhampi sīlaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā; sekhopi samādhi vutto bhagavatā, asekhopi samādhi vutto bhagavatā; sekhāpi paññā vuttā bhagavatā, asekhāpi paññā vuttā bhagavatā.
root_text.an3.73:2.4: Idha, mahānāma, bhikkhu sīlavā hoti pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati …pe… samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu.
root_text.an3.73:3.2: Idha, mahānāma, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.73:4.2: Idha, mahānāma, bhikkhu ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.73:5.1: Sa kho so, mahānāma, ariyasāvako evaṁ sīlasampanno evaṁ samādhisampanno evaṁ paññāsampanno āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.73:5.2: Evaṁ kho, mahānāma, sekhampi sīlaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā, asekhampi sīlaṁ vuttaṁ bhagavatā; sekhopi samādhi vutto bhagavatā, asekhopi samādhi vutto bhagavatā; sekhāpi paññā vuttā bhagavatā, asekhāpi paññā vuttā bhagavatā”ti.

translation_text.an3.73:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.an3.73:1.3: Now at that time the Buddha had recently recovered from an illness.
translation_text.an3.73:1.4: Then Mahānāma the Sakyan went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an3.73:1.5: “For a long time, sir, I have understood your teaching like this:
translation_text.an3.73:1.6: ‘Knowledge is for those with immersion, not those without immersion.’
translation_text.an3.73:1.7: But, sir, does immersion come first, then knowledge?
translation_text.an3.73:1.8: Or does knowledge come first, then immersion?”
translation_text.an3.73:1.10: “The Buddha has recently recovered from an illness,
translation_text.an3.73:1.11: and this Mahānāma asks him a question that’s too deep.
translation_text.an3.73:1.12: Why don’t I take him off to one side and teach him the Dhamma?”
translation_text.an3.73:2.1: Then Ānanda took Mahānāma by the arm, led him off to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an3.73:2.2: “Mahānāma, the Buddha has spoken of the ethics, immersion, and wisdom of a trainee; and the ethics, immersion, and wisdom of an adept.
translation_text.an3.73:2.3: What is the ethics of a trainee?
translation_text.an3.73:2.4: It’s when a mendicant is ethical, restrained in the monastic code, conducting themselves well and resorting for alms in suitable places. Seeing danger in the slightest fault, they keep the rules they’ve undertaken.
translation_text.an3.73:2.5: This is called the ethics of a trainee.
translation_text.an3.73:3.1: And what is the immersion of a trainee?
translation_text.an3.73:3.2: It’s when a mendicant, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption … second absorption … third absorption … fourth absorption.
translation_text.an3.73:3.3: This is called the immersion of a trainee.
translation_text.an3.73:4.1: And what is the wisdom of a trainee?
translation_text.an3.73:4.2: They truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.an3.73:4.3: This is called the wisdom of a trainee.
translation_text.an3.73:5.1: Then a noble disciple—accomplished in ethics, immersion, and wisdom—realizes the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an3.73:5.2: In this way the Buddha has spoken of the ethics, immersion, and wisdom of both a trainee and an adept.”
